channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: us

item.title: FBI says driver in New Orleans rampage acted alone, was '100%' inspired by Islamic State group
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — The U.S. Army veteran who drove a pickup truck into a crowd of New Year’s revelers acted alone, the FBI said Thursday, reversing its position from a day earlier that he likely worked with others in the deadly attack that officials said was inspired by the Islamic State group.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: Deputies are stuck using radios with Los Angeles County's dispatch system out of commission
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The archaic computer dispatch system for the nation's largest sheriff's department remained out of commission on Thursday after crashing on New Year's Eve, forcing deputies to handle all calls by radio, authorities said.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KREX 5

item.title: John Capodice, actor who appeared in 'Ace Ventura,' 'Seinfeld' and more, dies at 83
item.description: News of Capodice’s death on Monday was publicly announced in an obituary posted by the Pizzi Funeral Home in Northvale, New Jersey.
item.dc:creator: Michael Bartiromo, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: All Democrats expected to participate in Speaker’s vote
item.description: All Democrats are expected to be in the Capitol on Friday for the vote to seat the next Speaker of the House, according to the Democratic whip’s office.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, NewsNation

item.title: NYPD presence increased at Trump Tower, Times Square
item.description: “While there are no immediate threats to our city at this time, out of an abundance of caution, we have heightened security.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, NewsNation

item.title: New Orleans residents step up to donate blood after Bourbon Street attack
item.description: A blood drive is being held at the New Orleans Blood Center to support victims of the New Year's Day attack on Bourbon Street
item.dc:creator: Holly Galvan Posey, Gage Goulding, KPRC 2

item.title: Opening a savings account? Here's what to know
item.description: Annual percentage yields (APYs) on top high-yield savings accounts are currently 4.5% and above, according to Bankrate.
item.dc:creator: Andrew Dorn, NewsNation

item.title: Chick-fil-A celebrates the New Year with fan-favorite menu comebacks
item.description: Chick-fil-A is celebrating the New Year by bringing back old fan-favorite menu items. The popular chicken chain on Monday announced the return of Grilled Spicy Deluxe Sandwich and Key Lime seasonal beverages, which will be available at participating restaurants later this month.
item.dc:creator: Iman Palm, KTLA 5

item.title: What we know about the vehicle attack on New Year's Day in New Orleans
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Investigators now believe the driver who barreled through a crowd of New Year’s Day revelers in New Orleans acted alone and found he posted several videos on social media pledging his support for the Islamic State group, the FBI said Thursday.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Middle East latest: Israeli strikes across Gaza kill at least 26, including 3 kids
item.description: Israeli airstrikes killed at least 26 people across the Gaza Strip on Thursday, including three children and two high-ranking officers in the Hamas-run police force, according to Palestinian and hospital officials.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, PHL 17

item.title: FBI found 150 homemade bombs at Virginia home during search in December, prosecutors say
item.description: NORFOLK, Va. (AP) — Federal agents found one of the largest stockpiles of homemade explosives they have ever seized when they arrested a Virginia man on a firearms charge last month, according to a court filing by federal prosecutors.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Sugar Bowl security to equal Super Bowl: New Orleans police
item.description: The New Orleans Police Department revealed Thursday that the annual Sugar Bowl will feature heightened security after a man drove directly into a crowd on Bourbon Street earlier this week, leaving 15 dead.
item.dc:creator: Ashleigh Fields, Fox 5

item.title: Biden is giving the 2nd highest civilian award to the leaders of the Jan. 6 congressional committee
item.description: President Joe Biden is bestowing the second highest civilian medal on Liz Cheney and Bennie Thompson, leaders of the congressional investigation into the Capitol riot who Donald Trump has said should be jailed for their roles in the inquiry.
item.dc:creator: COLLEEN LONG, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: New Orleans attack victims: What we know so far
item.description: Investigations continue into an "act of terrorism" in New Orleans' bustling Canal and Bourbon streets after a truck drove into a crowd, killing 15 people and injuring at least 35 more.
item.dc:creator: Anna Kutz, WGN 9

item.title: Las Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver identified as active-duty soldier from Colorado: Sources
item.description: He allegedly served at the same military base as Shamsud-Din Jabbar — the man suspected of intentionally driving into a crowd in New Orleans, killing 15.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, Fox 5

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver identified as Army soldier from Colorado: source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Trump blames ‘open borders’ for New Orleans, Las Vegas attacks
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump blamed the situation at the U.S.-southern border for the recent attacks in New Orleans and Las Vegas, railing against President Joe Biden for his immigration policies as both cases are being investigated.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Cruise ship stomach bug illnesses hit 12-year high: CDC
item.description: Cruise ships saw the highest number of stomach bug illnesses since 2012 with 16 outbreaks impacting a total of 1,894 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, WFLA, NBC

item.title: Vegas Cybertruck explosion driver identified as Army soldier from Colorado: source
item.description: Livelsberger allegedly served at the same military base as the man suspected of driving into a crowd in New Orleans.
item.dc:creator: Travis Harrison, NewsNation

item.title: Trump Hotel guests complain of chaos following Cybertruck explosion
item.description: Guests staying at the Trump International Hotel were forced to evacuate Wednesday morning after a Tesla Cybertruck exploded at the main entrance, killing one person and injuring seven others.
item.dc:creator: Joshua Peguero, NewsNation

item.title: Borrowers cheer Biden's record-setting student loan forgiveness, but left bitter with the system
item.description: While grateful to see their loan balance disappear, borrowers who received forgiveness under Biden lament a system they say is holding back millions of others.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: 10 people shot outside New York City nightclub on New Year's Day: NYPD
item.description: At least 10 people were shot outside a Queens nightclub on New Year's Day, according to the New York City Police Department.
item.dc:creator: Erin Pflaumer, KRON4

item.title: Trump to hold DC rally day before inauguration
item.description: The president-elect has not held a rally in the District of Columbia since he delivered a speech on the Ellipse on Jan. 6, 2021, which preceded a violent attack on the Capitol by supporters attempting to stop the certification of the 2020 election results.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, KRON4

item.title: 10 people are wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub
item.description: Police say 10 people were wounded in a shooting outside a New York City nightclub while they were waiting to get into a private event.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Islamic State-inspired driver expressed desire to kill before deadly New Orleans rampage, Biden says
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — A U.S. Army veteran driving a pickup truck that bore the flag of the Islamic State group wrought carnage on New Orleans’ raucous New Year’s celebration, killing 15 people as he steered around a police blockade and slammed into revelers before being shot dead by police.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER, JIM MUSTIAN, KEVIN McGILL and JACK BROOK, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: NYE drone show canceled on Folly Beach after drones fall from sky; at least 1 injured
item.description: FOLLY BEACH, S.C. (WCBD) - A new drone show on Folly Beach was canceled Tuesday night after the drones malfunctioned and fell from the sky. Organizers completed two drone displays before they all went down, News 2 learned.
item.dc:creator: Tim Renaud, WGN 9

item.title: New Orleans New Year's Day ramming is the latest attack to use a vehicle as a deadly weapon
item.description: NEW ORLEANS (AP) — Police say a man who drove a pickup truck at high speed into a crowd of people celebrating the new year in New Orleans early Wednesday was hell-bent on creating carnage.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: FBI says it thwarted apparent plot against AIPAC
item.description: Law enforcement arrested Forrest Kendall Pemberton after he allegedly traveled to Plantation, Fla., in search of AIPAC’s office with several firearms, including an AR-15 rifle.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, KRON4
